title: rhamnus californica subsp. occidentalis
intro: Rhamnus californica subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western poison oak, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rhamnus californica subsp. occidentalis include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution should be exercised due to its toxic urushiol oil, which can cause skin irritation in some individuals.
